funding_references.funder_name: Italian Agency for Research on ALS-ARiSLA project 'Brindisys'
Italian Agency for Research on ALS

title: BNCI 2014-008 P300 dataset (ALS patients)
description: This dataset comprises EEG recordings from 8 patients with amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS) performing a P300-based brain-computer interface (BCI) speller task. Participants completed a copy-spelling paradigm using a 6×6 matrix with row-column intensification, generating 35 trials per subject across a single session. The preprocessed data, acquired at 256 Hz from 8 scalp electrodes, demonstrates high classification accuracy (97.5%) and represents a clinically relevant application of P300-based communication systems for severely paralyzed individuals.
methods_description: EEG data were recorded from 8 active electrodes (Fz, Cz, Pz, Oz, P3, P4, PO7, PO8) using g.MOBILAB hardware with g.Ladybird Ag/AgCl electrodes in a 10-10 montage. Signals were sampled at 256 Hz with online bandpass filtering (0.1-10 Hz) and 50 Hz notch filtering. Preprocessing included offline bandpass filtering (Butterworth, order 4), artifact rejection (amplitude threshold ±70 μV), and baseline correction using 200 ms pre-stimulus intervals. Classification employed stepwise linear discriminant analysis (SWLDA) with 7-fold cross-validation.
